title: Atriplex vulvaria
taxonomy: Atriplex vulvaria is classified as a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Amaranthaceae, commonly known as the pigweed or amaranth family. Within this family, Atriplex vulvaria is further classified as a genus of plants, which includes other saltbush species. Atriplex vulvaria is a perennial herb that is native to certain regions and is often found in coastal areas and salt lakes. It is a member of the order Caryophyllales, a group of flowering plants that includes other succulents and annuals. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Amaranthaceae family.
